title: Name already defined or imported
explanation: This error occurs when you import two items with the same name into the same scope. Rust does not allow name collisions in imports.
example_bad:
use std::fmt::Result;
use std::io::Result; // Error: `Result` already imported
error_message: error[E0252]: the name `Result` is defined multiple times

fixes.description: Use aliases with the as keyword
fixes.code:
use std::fmt::Result as FmtResult;
use std::io::Result as IoResult;

tip: The `as` keyword in use statements lets you rename imports to avoid conflicts. This is a common pattern with Result types.
